findings.description: The NCDENR logo/header graphic present at the top of the PDF page is missing from the HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the NCDENR tree/shield logo at the top of the page. HTML has no image, only text 'NCDENR' as a heading.

findings.description: Section I heading in PDF is underlined to denote it as a section heading. In HTML it uses a larger heading style but the underline formatting is different.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'I. Umbrella Mitigation Banking Instrument (UMBI) Template for Nutrient Offset Mitigation Banks' as underlined text. HTML renders it as a larger heading without underline.

findings.description: The PDF has a clear three-part header layout with Governor name on left, Director name center, Secretary name right. The HTML reproduces this but as a separate repeated block, creating visual duplication of the header area.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 has one header block. HTML shows the content rendered once in the main flow, then the footer information block appears separately below.

findings.description: The table of Corps' Regional General Permit Numbers and DWQ General Certification Numbers is rendered with proper structure in both formats, but the HTML uses a styled table with visible grid lines whereas the PDF uses underlined headers only.
findings.evidence: PDF table has minimal formatting with underlined column headers. HTML table has alternating rows with borders and header styling.

findings.description: Bullet/arrow markers in the PDF (➤ symbols) are converted to circle bullets in the HTML list items.
findings.evidence: PDF uses arrow/triangle bullet markers (➤) for list items under section C. HTML uses standard circle list markers (○).

findings.description: Italicized text blocks in the PDF (permit condition language) are preserved in the HTML rendering but rendered differently - some italic passages appear as regular text.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows several conditions in italic text (e.g., Construction Stormwater Permit section, Placement of Culverts section). HTML maintains some italic but not all.

findings.description: The h1 heading is 'NCDENR' which is the logo text, not the document title. The actual document subject line (the RE: field) or a descriptive title would better serve as the document title. However, this is an acceptable structural decision by the converter.
findings.evidence: HTML h1: 'NCDENR'. The document is a memo with RE: subject line.

findings.description: There is a nested <main> inside <main>, which is a structural HTML issue but does not affect content fidelity.

Alt text for all 7 embedded images is generic ('Scanned document page X') rather than descriptive of actual content. This is acceptable since the images appear to be scanned page images, but more descriptive alt text would be better.
findings.evidence: All img tags have alt='Scanned document page X' where X is 1-7

findings.description: The full scanned page images are embedded alongside the full text transcription, effectively duplicating all content - each page appears both as an image and as fully transcribed text. This is a design choice but results in significant content duplication.
findings.evidence: Each page of the PDF appears as both an tag and as full HTML text content.

findings.description: In section E, item i, the list numbering changes from alphabetic (a-h) to roman numeral (i) in the HTML. The PDF consistently uses lowercase alphabetic lettering (a through i).
findings.evidence: PDF: 'i. Any impacts to streams and buffers...' HTML: '<li style="list-style-type: lower-roman;">Any impacts to streams and buffers...'

findings.description: The PDF text says 'All application shall address' (singular, possibly a typo in the original), while the HTML says 'All applications shall address' (plural, corrected).
findings.evidence: PDF page 3: 'All applicationshall address stormwater management' (also missing space). HTML: 'All applications shall address stormwater management'
